Further classification of Pseudogynoxys westonii would involve its genus and species level classification within the Asteraceae family. intro: Pseudogynoxys westonii, also known as Western Waxflower, is a flowering plant species native to South America, specifically in the Andean regions of Peru and Ecuador. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Pseudogynoxys westonii include terpenes, sesquiterpenes, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
